Meaning: Champion identity & base info.
Clarify more: MERGED vertically across every row the champion owns, so the identity block spans all of that champion's steps. Origin/Class un-merged into separate slots. Range is the unit's attack range in HEXES, from tft-set9 → Champions → Range — a number, never a position. (It held 'Frontliner'/'Backliner' for 35 champions until round 4; see 'Note - Range'.)

Meaning: Order of actions in the skill.
Clarify more: Rows sharing a Step are one action — their cells are vertically merged.

Meaning: One Action producing multiple Effects.
Clarify more: When Step/Trigger/Action are merged across rows, those rows are the same attack — e.g. Kayle's Pierce Projectile both Damages and shreds MR.

Meaning: Active or Passive.
Clarify more: Active = cast on cooldown/mana; Passive = always-on / auto.

Meaning: The event that fires the Action. Use event that are life-cycle of the skill.
Clarify more: The full list lives in the 'Trigger Types' tab - that tab IS the vocabulary, and sync VALIDATE fails on any trigger used here that has no row there, so a trigger cannot be invented without adding it first (the user's call: 'I don't want you to add some random trigger'). Use an event in the skill's LIFE-CYCLE. Note 'After Cast' means IMMEDIATELY after the cast animation - if the action waits for something to run out, that is an Expire trigger instead (Warwick's stun waits out his 2.5s Attack Speed buff, so it is 'On Cast Expire', not 'After Cast').

Meaning: A state gate that must be true to do the Action.
Clarify more: Lvl 1-5 / Lvl 6-8 / Lvl 9+, If Target Wounded, If Already Transformed, If Ally Hit, If Ionia Active, Every 2/2/0 casts, If Empowered, If Already 3 Soldiers, If Target at Edge, If Target Not at Edge, If Ally Below 50% HP, If Kills Target, If Target Below 66% HP, If Target Marked, If 4-Star, If Killed By Skill, If Target NOT Below 66% HP. (Trigger = when; Condition = only-if.)

Meaning: WHO performs the action. Defaults to Self.
Clarify more: Self = the champion itself (almost every row). Summon = a unit it spawned acts on its own (Zed's Shadow, Azir's Sand Soldier). Ally = another unit entirely performs it (Sejuani's passive rides on an ALLY's attack). This is separate from Aim Target (what the action points at) and Effect Recipient (who the effect lands on) - three different questions, three columns. A source can also be a NON-champion actor: 'Step 1 Projectile' (Vel'Koz's split fires off his own first-hit bolt when it lands) and 'Child of the Star' (Soraka's summon).

Meaning: The Action the hero perform when the skill is working.
Clarify more: A KEY into the 'Action Model' tab — look the name up there to read how the action works (Apply / Spawn / Motion / Behavior / Shape). Those five axes lived here as their own Hero columns for one day and moved out: every one of them is decided by the action's NAME, so a per-row copy was ~200 rows restating a 23-row lookup and could only drift. What stays per-row is what genuinely varies for the same action: Collision, Offset, AOE (hex), Count, Spread. 'Auto-Attack' is NOT one of these when the champion attacks: that is the TRIGGER ('On Attack'), and the action is 'Bonus on AA'. Only a summon that really attacks is 'Summon Attack'.

Meaning: Gone from this tab — the ACTION decides it.
Clarify more: The geometry of the hitbox the action projects. It lived here per-row until every action turned out to have exactly one Collision: the last exception was Urgot, and he turned out to be a Cone AOE, not a Burst Projectile. Read it in the 'Action Model' tab. A champion whose collision differs from its action's does not need a column — it needs its own action (that is how Wave and Cone AOE happened).

Meaning: The range (in hexes) the aim target must be within for the skill to fire.
Clarify more: The skill does NOT cast the instant mana is full - the aim target must also be inside this range. Defaulted to the hero's auto-attack Range for now, to be refined per skill. Sits just before Aim Target.

Meaning: Who the Action aim at. This doesn't specify WHO the Effect was apply to though.
Clarify more: ABSOLUTE, never relative to the Action Source — 'Self' always means the champion, so a summon's own AOE is aimed at 'Summon' (Zed's shadow), NOT at 'Self'. Values: Current / Farthest / Farthest (within N hex) / Clustered / Self / Summon / Away from enemies / Lowest-HP Ally / Lowest-HP Allies / Lowest-HP enemy / Adjacent (both sides) / Knocked-up enemy / Nearest N enemies / Nearest enemy to the healed ally / Nearest enemy to owner / Step N Aim target (re-aims at whatever an earlier step aimed at — e.g. Yasuo's dash and slam both return to the enemy his Step 2 whirlwind picked) / Hex That Projectile Hit (an on-impact AOE centres on where the projectile LANDED, which is not the same as the unit it was aimed at).

Meaning: Who the Effect lands on.
Clarify more: Aimed enemy / First hit enemy / Enemies in area / Enemies in path / All enemies / Allies in path / Adjacent allies (left & right) / Self / Grabbed enemies / 2 lowest-HP allies. If the recipient is exactly the Aim Target, write 'Same to Aim Target' rather than restating it — the cell then only ever carries NEW information.

Meaning: Each Effect get separate into their own category.
Clarify more: See the 'Effect Types' tab, where the category column is merged into one block per category. There are SIX, not four: Movement and Summon are real and in use (14 rows between them) alongside Attack / Status / Buff / Debuff.

Meaning: This specify what the Effect does.
Clarify more: e.g. Status→Stun, Debuff→MR. Full list in 'Effect Types'.

Meaning: The number that the Effect was applied.
Clarify more: Numbers taken from tft-set9 → Champions → Skill Description, same per-star format (e.g. 200/300/450% AP). Scales this row's Effect Detail, not the whole skill.

Meaning: How the Amount behaves. The Amount value could be static or could be change dynamically depend on the condition.
Clarify more: stacking / decaying / falls-off per hit / +30% if Wounded / per spin / burst, etc.

Meaning: How often Effect is re-applies. e.g. damge once, damage over time, heal over time, etc...
Clarify more: Once / Every 0.5s / Every spin — the tick INTERVAL, not just 'Over Time' (the user's call: a periodic effect must say how often it ticks; pair it with Effect Duration for the total). This is the APPLICATION time of the effect - how the effect is dealt out, not how long it lasts. A permanent buff is applied ONCE and then never expires: that is Cadence = Once with Duration = Permanent, NOT a cadence of its own. ('Perm' was previously used here and was a category error.)

Meaning: How long the Effect lasts, in seconds.
Clarify more: Numeric seconds; per-star when it varies - e.g. Stun 1.5/2/8, Shred 3. 'Permanent' if it lasts the whole combat and never falls off (Viego's stacks, every Ionia bonus). Blank / em-dash if the effect is instant and has nothing to expire.

Meaning: Area radius in hexes.
Clarify more: For area actions only.

Meaning: Where the AOE sits relative to its centre (the source/target hex).
Clarify more: An enumerated anchor label along the facing line (centre→aim): 'centred' (shape centred on the origin), 'rear edge' (origin at the back edge, shape extends forward), 'front edge' (origin at the FRONT, shape extends BACKWARD from it — currently unused; note a cone is NOT this: its origin is at the narrow end and it grows forward, which is 'rear edge'), 'detached +N' (shape sits N hexes ahead, not touching the origin). '—' for non-AOE actions. The centre itself is derived from Action Source + Aim Target (Aim=Self → on the source, else on the target).

Meaning: Channel time of the Action, in seconds.
Clarify more: Sits at the END of the Action block (after Collision), not with the effects - it describes the ACTION, and one action has one cast time however many effects it produces, so it merges per action like the rest of that block. Rarely specified: only Galio (2), Garen (4) and Lux (3) have one; every other action reads '—'. It used to be the sheet's LAST column, marooned past the effect block, which is what made it read as an effect property.
